descriptions: Shining Path is affiliated with the Revolutionary Internationalist Movement.

edges.evidence_quote: Cosca ritenuta collegata al clan Moretti-Pellegrino-Lanza di Foggia.

descriptions: A Satanas member killed a Lemonwood Chiques member.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 1989 Manuel Rodriguez, a member of the Lemonwood Chiques gang, was shot and killed by Arnell Salagubang, a member of the Satanas gang.

descriptions: Aryan Nations Revival is a splinter group of Aryan Nations.
edges.evidence_quote: Based in New York, and led by Jay Faber, Aryan Nations Revival became one of the more well-known splinter groups.

descriptions: Hells Angels attacked the unsanctioned Windsor Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: On 15 April 1979, a group of up to thirty Hells Angels armed with guns, axes and knives ambushed fifteen members of the rogue Windsor Hells Angels who were sleeping in a car park near Brockenhurst.

descriptions: Mario Fabbrocino helped found the Onorata fratellanza.

descriptions: The Nuova Famiglia was formed from the earlier Onorata Fratellanza alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Alla fine del 1978 nasce la cosiddetta Nuova Famiglia , formatasi da una precedente alleanza denominata "Onorata Fratellanza"

descriptions: Matranga family extorted and intimidated Provenzano family, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: They eventually began moving in on Provenzano fruit loading operations intimidating them with threats of violence.

descriptions: The Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network supplies al-Shabaab with weapons and materials.
edges.evidence_quote: These pirates do, however, also supply ISS' rival in Puntland, al-Shabaab, with weapons and other materials.

descriptions: ISS cooperates with this pirate network for smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: In smuggling fighters and supplies across the Gulf of Aden, ISS works closely with Somali pirates, namely Mohamed Garfanje's Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network

descriptions: ISS cooperates with the Hobyo-Haradhere Piracy Network.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, it was revealed that the Islamic State – Somalia Province works closely with Somali pirates, namely Mohamed Garfanje's Hobyo - Haradhere Piracy Network and another unidentified group that is based in Qandala.

People Nation.description: The People Nation is an American alliance of street gangs based in Chicago, formed in 1978 by the Vice Lords, Black P. Stones, and Latin Kings. It is a multiethnic coalition involved in drug trafficking, robbery, money laundering, and murder. The alliance is known for using a five-pointed star and left-side identifiers, and it is a rival of the Folk Nation.

descriptions: BPSN is a member of the People Nation alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: part of the large Chicago gang alliance known as the People Nation

descriptions: Gaylords are a member of the People Nation alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang is part of the People Nation alliance

descriptions: The south side Popes joined the People Nation, while the north side is rival.
edges.evidence_quote: the south side branch... became a renegade faction, even going so far as to join the rival People Nation alliance

descriptions: Saints are rivals of People Nation (infobox lists as rival).
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , La Raza Nation , Gangster Two Six , Satan's Disciples , Latin Kings

descriptions: Saints joined People Nation for protection in prison.
edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1980s to early 1990s, many Saint gang members were incarcerated within the prison system which led the Saints to join the People Nation for protection.

descriptions: Almighty Vice Lord Nation is a founding member of People Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: It is a founding member of the People Nation multi-gang alliance.

descriptions: Bloods are affiliated with People Nation.

Importing Company, 317 Hanover St.

descriptions: Both Lanzino-Patitucci and Rango-Zingari received contraband from corrupt agents, but no direct relationship between the two orgs is stated.

'Ndrina Mammoliti.description: A powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Castellace and Oppido Mamertina in the Gioia Tauro plain, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1930s, it is involved in racketeering, kidnapping, extortion, and land expropriation. The clan is closely linked to the Rugolo clan through intermarriage and is considered one of the more powerful in the area. It has been active in feuds with rival clans such as the Barbaro 'ndrina.

descriptions: Barbaro 'ndrina fought a feud with Mammoliti 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1950s the clan was involved in a bloody feud with the Mammoliti 'ndrina

descriptions: The Barbaro 'ndrina fought a feud with the Mammoliti 'ndrina in the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1950s the clan was involved in a bloody feud with the Mammoliti 'ndrina from Castellace in the municipality of Oppido Mamertina.

descriptions: Barbaro and Mammoliti were opposing sides in the Faida di Castellace.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Castellace faida nata nel 1954 e durata fino al 1978 tra i Barbaro e i Mammoliti

descriptions: The Barbaro were involved in a feud with the Mammoliti in the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Cinquanta, quando risiedevano ancora a Castellace furono coinvolti in una faida con i Mammoliti .

descriptions: Mammoliti supported Ferraro-Raccosta during the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2003, ormai in carcere, Saverio Mammoliti si dissocia dall'organizzazione e confessa che durante la faida i Mammoliti dettero l'appoggio ai Ferraro-Raccosta

descriptions: Mammoliti sided with Ferraro-Raccosta in the faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011 sembra riesplosa la faida tra le due consorterie e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Antonino Mammoliti acted as guarantor for the Imerti clan during peace negotiations.
edges.evidence_quote: Antonino Mammoliti fu il garante per gli Imerti

descriptions: Mammoliti is a clan within the 'Ndrangheta organization.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mammoliti 'ndrina ... is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Mammoliti and Barbaro were engaged in a violent feud in the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: Feud with the Barbaro clan ... In the 1950s the Mammoliti clan was involved in a bloody feud in Castellace with the Barbaro 'ndrina.

descriptions: Mammoliti and Barbaro were in a violent feud (faida) starting in the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Cinquanta scoppia una faida con i Barbaro di Castellace .

descriptions: Mammoliti supported the Ferraro-Raccosta group during the faida of Oppido Mamertina.
edges.evidence_quote: i Mammoliti dettero l'appoggio ai Ferraro-Raccosta .

descriptions: Mammoliti sided with Ferraro-Raccosta in the renewed conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 2011, la battaglia tra le due consorterie sembra riesplosa, e questa volta sarebbero stati coinvolti anche i Mammoliti a favore di questi ultimi.

descriptions: Mammoliti, Nirta Scalzone, and Raso were part of the same criminal group in Aosta.
edges.evidence_quote: arrestate 16 persone di un sodalizio operanti come Locale di Aosta almeno dal 2014 e riconducibili ai Nirta Scalzone , ai Raso e ai Mammoliti

descriptions: Mammoliti-Rugolo is allied with the Mazzaferro 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mammoliti-Rugolo è alleata dei Piromalli , Varone e dei Mazzaferro .

descriptions: Mammoliti-Rugolo clan is closely linked to Piromalli 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mammoliti-Rugolo clan is closely linked to the Piromalli 'ndrina

descriptions: Mammoliti supported De Stefano against Tripodo.
edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefanos won a monopoly ... with the support of the Piromalli and Mammoliti 'ndrine

descriptions: The Mammoliti cosca supported the De Stefano clan after the war.
edges.evidence_quote: They won a monopoly of construction work in northern Reggio Calabria, moving the rival Tripodo group out of the market of public work contracts with the support of the Piromalli and Mammoliti cosche.

descriptions: Mammoliti supported the new faction alongside De Stefano.
edges.evidence_quote: la posizione di Piromalli venne appoggiata dalle nuove leve, come i De Stefano di Reggio Calabria , ma anche dai Mammoliti di Oppido Mamertina e Castellace

descriptions: Longo supported Piromalli in their conflict against Mammoliti.
edges.evidence_quote: i Longo hanno appoggiato i Piromalli, loro vecchi alleati, nella lotta per il potere nella Piana di Gioia Tauro che li ha visti contrapporsi ai Mammoliti

descriptions: Alvaro was in conflict with Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo during the faida di Sinopoli.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli Alvaro, appoggiati dalle famiglie Forgione e Violi, per vent'anni dovettero regolare i conti con i Filleti-De Angelis-Orfeo.

descriptions: The U.S. chapter of the Muslim Brotherhood was started by MSA activists.
edges.evidence_quote: the U.S. chapter of Muslim Brotherhood was started by activists involved with the Muslim Students Association (MSA).

Sons of Satan Motorcycle Club.description: One percenter outlaw motorcycle club based in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, founded in 1949 by returning WWII veterans. Originally a family-oriented club, it was transformed into a violent gang under John 'Satan' Marron, who later became National President of the Pagan's Motorcycle Club. The club operates as a brother club to the Pagans, wearing a diamond 'P' patch to signify allegiance. Its clubhouse was destroyed by a pipe bomb in 2002, suspected to be the work of the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Sons of Satan are listed as a rival motorcycle gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival motorcycle gangs include the ... Sons of Satan

descriptions: The Hells Angels are suspected of bombing the Sons' clubhouse, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Although authorities believe it to be the work of rival motorcycle gang, the Hells Angels , the case has yet to be officially solved.

descriptions: The Pagans' expansion led to a brief conflict with the Sons.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, The Pagans began moving north into Central Pennsylvania which sparked a brief conflict between the two clubs.

descriptions: The Sons are a brother club to the Pagans, indicating a cooperative alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Sons of Satan Motorcycle Club , known locally as The Sons , is a one percenter outlaw motorcycle club and brother club for the Pagan's Motorcycle Club .

descriptions: After the conflict, the Sons came under Pagan oversight, a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: The conflict was resolved when Sons of Satan President John Marron and Pagans' President Fred 'Dutch' Burhans formally met and became close friends. Marron and a few select Sons members soon moved over to the Pagans while the remaining Sons were allowed to continue their club under Pagan oversight.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan was in conflict with the Stabile clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lo Russo clan, for its good organization, also had a leading role in a conflict with the Stabile

descriptions: Stabile clan is a member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Apart from the Licciardi clan, the alliance included the Contini, Mallardo, Lo Russo, Stabile, Prestieri, Bocchetti and Di Lauro clans.

descriptions: Stabile and Lo Russo, formerly allies, fought a conflict in 2003-2004.
edges.evidence_quote: faida di Chiaiano : conflitto svoltosi nel corso del 2003 e 2004 a Chiaiano tra il clan Stabile e il clan Lo Russo, in precedenza alleati sotto la bandiera dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: A founding member of Dixon Bloods was allegedly a former member of G-Shine Bloods, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Unconfirmed rumours state that one of the founding members of the gang was a former member of a New York-based gang known as G-Shine Bloods, who had relocated to the neighborhood in the early 2000s.

descriptions: The clan is a constituent part of the Serbian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: El Clan de Zemun ... es uno de los clanes de Belgrado de la Mafia serbia.

descriptions: LPK was a precursor that played a crucial role in creating the KLA.
edges.evidence_quote: A key precursor to the Kosovo Liberation Army was the People's Movement of Kosovo (LPK).

descriptions: Pizzimenti is listed as an allied 'ndrina in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Pizzimenti

Bowe Brothers.description: The Bowe Brothers were a criminal family in New York City during the early-to-mid-19th century, headed by Martin Bowe. The gang operated along the New York waterfront, engaging in theft, robbery, and fencing stolen goods. They were known for raiding dockyards and ships anchored in the East River. The group was active from approximately 1840 to 1860.

Clerkenwell crime syndicate.description: English criminal organization based in Clerkenwell, London, founded in the 1980s by Terry Adams and his brothers Tommy and Patrick. Involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other serious crimes. Allegedly one of the most powerful syndicates in the UK with wealth up to £200 million. Influence has decreased since 2000.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Adams family as an ally of the Arifs.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Adams family , Turkish mafia

descriptions: Tommy Adams, a member of the syndicate, gained an $80 million credit line from Colombian drug cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: Tommy Adams is suspected of establishing connections to other international criminal organisations including ... gaining an $80 million credit line from Colombian drug cartels.

descriptions: The Adams family ordered the shooting of a former enforcer of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The shooting of the then 68-year-old "Mad" Frankie Fraser, a former enforcer for The Richardson Gang, in July 1991 was said to have been ordered by the Adams family

descriptions: The Adams family is believed to have connections with South American drug cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: The family is believed to have connections with various criminal organisations, specifically with South American drug cartels.

descriptions: Tommy Adams, a member of the syndicate, is suspected of establishing connections with Yardie gangs.

'Ndrina Polimeni.description: A 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Italy. It is allied with the Mazzagatti and Bonarrigo clans. The group has been involved in a long-running feud with the Ferraro-Raccosta clan, with violent escalations in the 1990s and again in the 2010s. The 2013 operation Erinni led to arrests and asset seizures, potentially ending the conflict.

Hogan Gang.description: The Hogan Gang was a St. Louis-based criminal organization active during Prohibition, involved in bootlegging, labor slugging, voter intimidation, armed robbery, and murder. Founded around 1917 by Edward 'Jelly Roll' Hogan, the gang was predominantly Irish-American but included Italian and Jewish members. They fought a violent gang war with Egan's Rats in the early 1920s and were active from the 1920s to the 1930s.

descriptions: Egan's Rats fought a war with the Hogan Gang after suspecting them of murdering Willie Egan.
edges.evidence_quote: Egan's Rats soon went to war with the rival Hogan Gang, whom they suspected of killing Willie Egan.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and the Hogan Gang fought the Egan-Hogan War.
edges.evidence_quote: The gangs engaged in the Egan-Hogan War of 1921–23, which resulted in the breakup of Egan's Rats.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Cuckoo Gang as an ally of the Hogan Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cuckoo Gang , Russo Gang

descriptions: The Hogan Gang and Egan's Rats were engaged in a violent gang war.

descriptions: The Hogan Gang had a close business partnership with the Russo Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: they had a close business partnership with the Russo Gang
